highlights.text: I wrote on Monday at 4pm (before the sell) that: “ Bear case tomorrow: Short-term, begins below 6453” and “If 6453 fails, we start down.” Tuesday morning, ES lost 6453 and began a sell that took ES down to 6362 by yesterday morning.
As readers know very well though, every “elevator down” sell in ES (which is what we saw yesterday morning) has a sibling: The short squeeze.
When ES sells, it violently cuts through every level for a few minutes to a few hours, it induces panic, then it puts in a vertical squeeze on a Failed Breakdown.

In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll discuss some of the quality setups we’ve seen this week as we’ve seen all of them from my system: Failed Breakdowns to starts the week, a rare “Breakdown Short” Tuesday to sell us lower, then another Failed Breakdown yesterday to squeeze us up.
They need to recover some key resistances.” We generally spent all day today in consolidation, holding that 6420 high from yesterday.

post_title: Jackson Hole Tomorrow. Expect Volatility In SPX. Can Bulls Hold The Low? August 22 Plan
